original: English authorBeatrix Potterwrites the story of Peter Rabbit for a 5-year-old boy

US 4 x 100 m medley relay team of Mike Stamm, Tom Bruce,Mark Spitz, and Jerry Heidenreich sets a world record of 3:48.16 to win gold at the Munich Olympics; Mark Spitz becomes the first athlete to win seven Olympic gold medals at a single Games
George Eastmanpatents the first roll-film camera and registers the brand name "Kodak"
In its first large-scale test,Thomas Edison's light bulb is used to light New York's Pearl Street Station
FBI announces it has recovered a pair of Dorothy's ruby slippers from "The Wizard of Oz," stolen 13 years earlier
A "magic carpet" that can help prevent falls among the elderly by warning them of unusual footsteps is developed by researchers at the University of Manchester
MLB New York Yankees one-handed pitcher Jim Abbott no-hits Cleveland Indians 4-0 at Yankee Stadium
Google is formally incorporated byLarry PageandSergey Brin, two students at Stanford University
400-year-old sunken Portuguese spice trade ship discovered near the port of Cascais, Portugal
Lagos' first-ever metro rail service, the Blue Line, begins operation in Nigeria, reducing a 2-hour journey to 15 minutes
Californian skier Scott Michael Pellaton sets barefoot waterski speed record at 119.36 mph (191.51 km/h)
After blowing an 11-run lead to Kansas City, Scott Hatteberg's bottom of the 9th walk-off home run gives Oakland a historic MLB victory, 12-11; A's set an AL record 20-game winning streak

Argentina pulls off one of the biggest upsets in basketball history, beating the US 87-80 in the second round of the World Championships; first US loss in 59 games since sending NBA players to international competition in 1992
Soviet sprinter Valeriy Borzov runs 20.00 to win the men's 200 m gold medal at the Munich Olympics, wrapping up the 100/200 m double at the Games
Archaeological remains of a Viking fortress from the 900s CE, Vallø Borgring, are discovered in Denmark

British-Kenyan aviator Beryl Markham takes off from Abingdon in England on a solo flight across the Atlantic Ocean and crash-lands at Baleine Cove on Cape Breton Island, Nova Scotia, Canada, becoming the first woman to make the east-west trip
First airplane to exceed 300 mph (483 kph), J.R. Wendell, Glenview, IL

16-year-old Ulrike Meyfarth of West Germany, with a world record-equalling 1.92 m, wins the women's high jump gold medal at the Munich Olympics; she is the youngest winner of an individual athletics event in Olympic history
Peter B. Cortese of the US achieves a one-arm deadlift of 370 lbs, 22 lbs over triple his body weight, in York, Pennsylvania
Lewis Howard Latimer, American inventor (carbon filament lightbulbs), born in Chelsea, Massachusetts (d. 1928)
KingCharles IIof England, on the run from Parliamentarians at White Ladies Priory, disguises himself as a countryman by rubbing his hands and face with soot
Rock group The Who's van is vandalized with $10,000 worth of equipment stolen while the group is inside Battersea Dogs Home buying a guard dog at the time
Willem Ruska of the Netherlands wins gold medals in both the heavyweight and open class Olympic judo competitions in Munich, becoming the first judoka to win two Olympic gold medals at one Games

American swimmer Melissa Belote, with a world record of 2:19.19, wins the 200 m backstroke gold medal at the Munich Olympics and wraps up the backstroke double, having won the 100 m in Olympic record time
Carlette Guidry-White, American athlete (Olympic gold 4 x 100m relay 1992, 1996; World C'ships 1995), born in Houston, Texas
Longest MLB game at Fenway Park ends in 20 innings as Seattle Mariners beat Boston Red Sox 8-7
Chinese diver Fu Mingxia wins the 10 m platform gold medal at the World Swimming Championships in Rome, Italy, becoming the first female to win back-to-back world titles in the event
Miami quarterbackDan Marinothrows five touchdown passes in Dolphins' 39-35 win over New England; sets NFL record with his 18th game of four or more touchdown passes
Bevo Francis, American basketball forward (NCAA record for points scored in a game [113] 1954–2012), born in Hammondsville, Ohio (d. 2015)
Joan Aiken, English writer of children's books (The Whispering Mountain), born in Rye, Sussex, England (d. 2004)
Russian ballerina Natalia Makarova defects to the West while on tour with the Kirov Ballet in London and is granted political asylum
Thieves steal 18 paintings from the Montreal Museum of Fine Arts, the largest art theft in North America at the time
N. K. Jemisin wins her fifth Hugo Award for her graphic novel Far Sector (Best Graphic Story or Comic), making her one of the few authors to achieve this

Long-lost painting, 'Portrait of a Lady' by Giuseppe Ghislandi, is handed over to the Argentine justice system after being discovered in an estate that belonged to the daughter of a Nazi fugitive; the painting was looted in Nazi-occupied Europe, missing for 80 years
Max Delbrück, German-American biologist and molecular geneticist (bacteriophage, Nobel Prize 1969), born in Berlin, Germany (d. 1981)
Phill Lewis, American actor (The Suite Life of Zack & Cody - "Mr. Moseby"), and director, born in Uganda
Eduard Khil, Russian baritone pop singer ("I Am Very Glad, As I Am Finally Returning Back Home"), sometimes known as "Mr. Trololo", born in Smolensk, Russia, USSR (d. 2012)
Australian Jack Brabham clinches his third Formula 1 World Drivers' Championship with a sixth place in the Italian Grand Prix at Monza
Tom Tupa scores the first two-point conversion in NFL history, running a fake extra point attempt for the Cleveland Browns in a 28-20 win at Cincinnati
20-year-oldKyle Buschbecomes the youngest driver to win a NASCAR Cup Series race when he outduels Greg Biffle in the Sony HD 500 at California Speedway
Mother Teresais canonized byPope Francisin a ceremony at the Vatican
The Beatles' single "Help!" goes to #1 and stays #1 for three weeks
John LennonandYoko Onoperform three songs ("Imagine," "Now or Never," "Give Peace a Chance") atJerry Lewis's 7th Muscular Dystrophy Telethon at the Americana Hotel, New York City
Cult TV series "Xena: Warrior Princess," starringLucy Lawlessand Renee O'Connor, debuts in the US
"Beetle Bailey" comic strip debuts in 12 newspapers
Apache ChiefGeronimosurrenders, ending the last major US-Indian war

British Association for the Advancement of Science proposes standard units of electrical measurement (the Volt afterAlessandro Volta; the Ohm afterGeorg Ohm) at its 31st annual meeting (Manchester, England)
Al-Biruni, Iranian mathematician, astronomer and polymath, born in Kath, Khwarezm, Afrighid dynasty (d. 1048) [some sources cite 5th as birth date]
Julian W. Hill, American research chemist (developed nylon), born in St. Louis, Missouri (d. 1996)
Strongest typhoon to hit Japan in 25 years, Jebi hits Tokushima Prefecture, swamping Kansai Airport and killing at least 11
A 7.1 magnitude earthquake strikes the South Island of New Zealand at 4:35 am, causing widespread damage and several power outages in the Canterbury region
Monsoon rains begin and cause flooding over a sustained period, killing over 400 people in India and Pakistan
Storm Daniel begins across Greece, Turkey, and Bulgaria, killing more than a dozen people as some regions receive 800 mm (31.5 inches) of rain, more than a year's worth of precipitation
Joseph McNeil, American USAF Major-General, and civil rights advocate (Greensboro Four lunch counter sit-in, 1960), dies of complications of Parkinson's disease at 83
Scottish Forth Road Bridge opens as the longest in Europe at the time
Maiden flight of the first US airship, USS Shenandoah
NASCAR's first paved superspeedway, Darlington Raceway, hosts the Southern 500, the first 500-mile event in NASCAR history; Johnny Mantz wins in a Plymouth
RKO Radio Pictures releases "Swing Time," directed by George Stevens and starringFred AstaireandGinger Rogers
Queen Wilhelminaabdicates the Dutch throne in favor of her daughter Juliana due to illness after 58 years, the longest reign of any Dutch monarch
Kelly Clarksonis crowned the winner of the first season of the "American Idol" TV singing competition series
